title: Complaints | PoliceStationRepUK — Find Accredited Police Station Reps

headings.text: Need a Police Station Rep in Kent?

content: HomeComplaints ProcedureComplaints ProcedureLast updated: 4 January 2026About This Complaints ProcedurePoliceStationRepUK is operated by Defence Legal Services Ltd.This procedure covers complaints about:The directory website and platformInaccurate information in listingsData protection concernsCustomer service or supportImportant:This procedure does not cover complaints about the conduct or service quality of individual representatives. If you have a complaint about a representative's professional conduct, you should contact their supervising firm or the Solicitors Regulation Authority (SRA).How to Make a ComplaintTo make a complaint about the directory service, please contact us:Email: robertcashman@defencelegalservices.co.ukPhone: 07535 494446Please include:Your name and contact detailsDescription of the complaintWhat you would like us to do to resolve itAny relevant screenshots or evidenceOur Response ProcessStep 1: Acknowledgement (within 2 working days)We will acknowledge receipt of your complaint and assign it a reference number.Step 2: Investigation (within 7 working days)We will investigate your complaint and gather relevant information.Step 3: Resolution (within 14 working days)We will provide a full response explaining our findings and any action we will take.Urgent complaints: If your complaint concerns data protection or inaccurate information that could cause harm, please mark it as "URGENT" and we will prioritize it.Specific Complaint TypesInaccurate Representative InformationIf a representative's profile contains inaccurate information (e.g., wrong phone number, closed firm, false accreditation claims), we will:Contact the representative to verify detailsTemporarily suspend the profile if we cannot reach themRemove the profile if information cannot be verifiedData Protection ComplaintsIf your complaint relates to how we handle personal data, you also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO): ico.org.uk/make-a-complaintProfessional Conduct ComplaintsIf you have concerns about a representative's professional conduct or service quality, this should be directed to:Their supervising solicitor firm (if instructed through a firm)The Solicitors Regulation Authority (SRA) if they are a solicitorThe firm who instructed them (not the directory)PoliceStationRepUK does not regulate representatives and is not responsible for their conduct.Make a ComplaintContact Defence Legal Services LtdEmail Complaint
